被害者のPC上でVSCodeを起動してGitHubの認証コードを発行させ、そのコードを攻撃者が横取りすることで、ブラウザ越しに被害者PCを遠隔操作する手口が報告されています。VSCodeにはブラウザ経由で端末を操作できる正規の「トンネル（Remote-Tunnels）」機能があり、MITRE-ATT&CKにも登録済みで中国系APTなどによる悪用が報告されてきた既知の手法ですが、北朝鮮系Kimsukyが2026年上半期に展開したスピアフィッシング4キャンペーンのうち1件でこれを用いていたことが報告されました。  4件は標的も初期手口も異なるものの、正規ツールやサービスを悪用するLotL（環境寄生）型の共通フレームワーク上で運用されており、標的は採用担当者から暗号通貨関係者、防衛当局者、大学院関係者まで広がっているとのことです。  個別には既知のLotL手口ながら、IoCが短期間で入れ替わるうえ正規ツール・サービスに紛れるため、LNKやJSEの二重拡張子検出、スケジューラの正規サービス偽装名の監視、正規CDN宛の不審なスクリプト取得といった行為ベースの検知が有効と報告されています。  【要点の整理】  ・4件中3件はPDF偽装のLNKファイル、1件は韓国語文書偽装の二重拡張子JSE（[.]hwpx[.]jse）を使用。囮文書を表示しながら裏でペイロードを展開する共通手法。  ・あるキャンペーンでは感染5分以内にUAC無効化、Defender例外登録、タスクスケジューラへの永続化までを一括処理。スケジューラ名はIntelのネットワークドライバ名に偽装され、5分周期の監視と5秒周期の命令受信が二重稼働する。  ・4件に共通するのは正規OSツールを悪用するLotL手法で、curlやcertutil、rundll32などが用いられている。加えて一部のキャンペーンではGitHubやMicrosoft-CDN、VSCodeトンネルといった正規サービスも悪用しており、いずれも単体では既知ながら、この環境寄生型の枠組みを4件が共有している点が今回の整理の眼目とされる。  ・被害者の識別にはuid、IPアドレス、MACアドレスを使い分け、MACをシードにターゲット環境にのみペイロードを返すことで分析環境を回避。  詳細は以下を参照： logpresso.com/ko/blog/2026-0…

A YouTuber claims the Trump Mobile site is exposing customer information through a basic security exploit.  Voidzilla says his mailing address, email, and order details were accessible, but not credit card info.  The alleged leak may also suggest Trump Mobile’s real order numbers are far lower than previously reported.  The Trump Mobile T1 Phone may finally be moving from punchline to actual product, but apparently, this launch still had room for another clownish twist.     After months of delays and general weirdness, the $499 phone has started reaching some media outlets, but a YouTuber claims the Trump Mobile website is leaking customer information.    Voidzilla, a YouTuber with 1.55 million subscribers, says in a new video that Trump Mobile’s website is exposing customer data through what he describes as a simple security exploit.     He says he is one of the affected customers, with his mailing address, email address, and order information accessible, though not his credit card details.     The same alleged exposure may also point to a much lower number of real Trump Mobile orders than previously claimed.    youtu.be/voxXDDq58Bk

🚨INTEL BRIEF: APT28 AND THE 2026 MIDTERMS  Actor: APT28 / Fancy Bear / Forest Blizzard / STRONTIUM  Attribution: Russian GRU Unit 26165 (85th Main Special Service Center)  Confidence: High  Date: 19 May 2026  Analyst Assessment: APT28 is a credible and persistent threat to the 2026 U.S. midterm cycle, especially for phishing, credential theft, access operations, and leak-enabled influence activity. Their most likely impact is not a single major hack, but repeated low- to moderate-intensity intrusions that create political leverage when paired with timing and amplification.  EXECUTIVE ASSESSMENT  APT28 remains operationally relevant because the group continues to exploit the same access paths that political and election ecosystems often leave exposed: weak identity controls, public-facing systems, vendor relationships, and user-driven phishing. The group’s documented tradecraft includes spearphishing, password spraying, valid accounts, and exploitation of public-facing applications, and current reporting shows continued exploitation of vulnerable routers and fresh campaigns leveraging current vulnerabilities.  For the midterms, the highest-probability outcome is targeted access against campaigns, consultants, vendors, and election-adjacent organizations, followed by selective use of stolen material to support influence narratives.  MISSION RELEVANCE  APT28’s value in an election cycle is not just collection; it is turning access into narrative effect. That means they are most dangerous when stolen emails, internal documents, or credentialed access can be released or hinted at in ways that shape trust, generate confusion, or embarrass targets at a critical time.  They are especially relevant where organizations rely on external vendors, shared mail systems, weak MFA, or delayed patching.  OBSERVED TTPs  - Spearphishing attachments and links. - Password spraying and credential validation. - Valid account abuse after credential theft. - Exploitation of public-facing applications and webmail systems. - Cloud and web-based exfiltration. - Opportunistic exploitation of routers and edge devices for persistence or credential collection.  2026 MIDTERM RISK  Most likely target set: - Campaign staff and volunteers. - State and local party infrastructure. - Consultants, pollsters, and vendors. - Election-adjacent IT and web service providers. - Local government users with broad access but weak security controls.  Most likely effects: - Account takeover. - Document theft. - Staged leaks timed for political effect. - Credential reuse across third-party systems. - Influence support through selective release and narrative shaping.  Probability judgment: APT28 activity in the cycle is likely; public, unmistakable, attribution-heavy disruption is less certain. Their preferred style is to stay useful, not necessarily obvious.  INDICATORS TO WATCH  - Spike in phishing against campaign and vendor mailboxes. - Password-spray activity against Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, VPN, and admin portals. - Login anomalies from unusual geographies, devices, or repeated valid-account use. - New compromise reports involving routers, edge devices, or webmail servers. - Leaks or anonymous dumps arriving with suspiciously precise political timing.  A key warning pattern is the combination of access plus selective release plus sudden narrative uptake.  DEFENSIVE PRIORITY  - Enforce MFA, preferably phishing-resistant MFA for privileged accounts. - Patch public-facing systems and internet-facing appliances quickly. - Review vendor and consultant access paths. - Audit mailbox rules, forwarding, and unusual OAuth grants. - Increase logging and alerting on account anomalies. - Run pre-election phishing simulations and incident-response drills.  BOTTOM LINE  APT28 should be treated as a high-confidence, high-relevance election threat actor for 2026.

The situation involving Nirvana Nonkwe and Bonko Khoza exposes a systemic vulnerability within the film industry: the critical need for absolute professionalism regarding performance boundaries.   Acting is an intricate, highly specialized craft. Before cameras roll, scripts are analyzed, scenes are dissected, boundaries are negotiated, and roles are explicitly consented to.  Actors have every right—and should be empowered—to decline a role or demand script modifications if a scene compromises their comfort or emotional well-being.  However, a dangerous cultural shift occurs when public discourse weaponizes serious accusations without separating the actor from the character.   Reducing an actor to a "pervert" simply because their performance was raw, convincing, or emotionally intense completely misinterprets the objective of the craft, which is to evoke belief.  Concurrently, the industry bears an undeniable responsibility to safeguard sets through unwavering communication, explicit consent protocols, and professional direction. Intense emotional residue is a real consequence of handling sensitive narratives, but these instances require institutional care and nuance, not immediate public trials driven by outrage.   For South Africa’s film industry to command global respect, acting must be treated as a serious profession. Great acting demands vulnerability, but conflating a difficult scene with abuse, or an emotional aftermath with misconduct, undermines justice.   Serious allegations demand a rigorous commitment to facts, systemic professionalism, and fairness for every party involved.

[언론보도] 스틸리언, AI 취약점 분석 제품 에일리언레이(AlienRay) 출시 v.daum.net/v/202605200949178… #스틸리언 #STEALIEN #사이버보안 #미토스 #Mythos #AI #인공지능 #AlienRay  - 소스코드 없이 윈도우·모바일 소프트웨어 취약점 분석 - 공급망 보안 대응 위한 블랙박스 기반 AI 분석기술 적용 - "미토스 충격에 맞서 출시, 실제 위협에 초점 맞춰 설계"  사이버 보안 기업 스틸리언은 AI 기반 취약점 분석 솔루션 ‘에일리언레이(AlienRay)’의 첫 제품군을 출시했다고 20일 밝혔다.  이번 에일리언레이(AlienRay) 윈도우 및 모바일을 시작으로 향후 다양한 제품군을 순차 확대할 예정이다.  현재 에일리언레이는 금융권과 공공기관을 대상으로 다수의 PoC(Proof of Concept)를 진행 중이다. 일부 대형 금융사와 공공부문 고객사들은 이미 도입을 확정했다. 특히 공급망 보안 관점에서 내·외부로부터 도입한 각종 소프트웨어 자산의 취약점을 자체적으로 점검할 수 있다는 점이 관심을 받고 있다.  박찬암 스틸리언 대표이사는 “최근 미토스로 인한 국가 차원의 보안 우려가 높아진 것이 사실”이라며 “AI 기반 보안기술이 우리나라 사이버 안보에 역할을 할 수 있도록 노력하겠다”고 말했다.  📰관련기사 - 이데일리 (2026. 05. 20)

Windows 취약점 폭증과 금융권 API 위기: 동맹 밖의 기업이 생존하는 법 📷 5월 3주차 보안 경보: 무너지는 인프라 신뢰성   2026년 5월 18일 현재, 글로벌 사이버 보안의 전쟁터는 그 어느 때보다 뜨겁습니다.  미국 CISA가 윈도우 핵심 취약점의 실제 악용을 경고하고, 글로벌 서버 공급망까지 흔들리는 지금, 특히 금융권과 엔터프라이즈 인프라를 타깃으로 한 AI해킹은 인간 방어자의 속도를 완전히 초월했습니다.  글로벌 보안 동맹 '글래스윙'이 그들만의 실시간 패치 성벽을 쌓는 동안, 동맹 밖에 놓인 대한민국 기업들은 어떤 무기로 이 거대한 위협에 맞서야 할까요?  1. AI가 주도하는 '익스플로잇 체인'의 공포 최근 CISA가 경고한 Windows MSHTML 취약점(CVE-2024-30051) 사례는 해커들이 AI를 어떻게 활용하는지 적나라하게 보여줍니다. - 취약점의 결합: 해커는 단 하나의 강력한 제로데이를 찾지 않습니다. 영리한 AI 에이전트가 방치된 구형 취약점 3~4개를 엮어 순식간에 시스템 최고 권한(Admin)을 탈취하는 '체인형 공격'을 감행합니다. - API 비즈니스 로직 타깃: 금융권 API의 허점을 노린 자율형 봇넷은 탐지 시스템을 우회하여 계정을 탈취하고 한도를 우회하는 등, 즉각적인 재무적 손실을 입히고 있습니다.  2. 거대 클라우드 AI의 치명적 한계와 '미토스'의 그림자 많은 기업이 보안 진단을 위해 외부 클라우드 AI 도구를 검토하지만, 금융권과 공공기관에서는 태생적으로 도입이 불가능합니다. - 소스코드 유출: 기업의 심장부인 소스코드와 API 명세서를 외부 클라우드로 전송하는 순간, 그 자체가 또 다른 공급망 유출의 통로가 됩니다. - 통제 불가능한 비용: 수천만 건의 자산을 종량제 토큰 비용을 내며 클라우드로 스캔하는 것은 예산의 재앙입니다.  3. 독자적 자강(自强)의 유일한 해답, 'MOMUS AI' 디포트리스가 개발한 MOMUS AI는 클라우드 연결을 완전히 끊은 100% 온프레미스 기반 자율 모의해킹 시스템입니다. 미토스(Mythos)급 AI 공격에 맞설 수 있는 국내 유일의 독자적 파수꾼입니다. - ReAct 자율 추론 엔진: 단순한 시그니처 대조 스캐너가 아닙니다. AI가 스스로 관찰하고, 추론하고, 우회 공격 계획을 짜며 우리 시스템의 '익스플로잇 체인'을 먼저 찾아내 부숩니다. - 데이터 유출 Zero (Local Air-Gap): 듀얼 GTX 3090 온프레미스 인프라를 기반으로, 외부망과의 연결 없이 전산실 내부에서만 완벽한 데이터 주권을 유지하며 구동됩니다. - C-Level 재무 리스크 리포트: 취약점을 방치했을 때 발생할 개인정보보호법(PIPA)상의 매출액 10% 과징금 리스크를 재무적 수치(₩)로 환산하여 경영진의 합리적 의사결정을 지원합니다.  4. 최후의 보루: "핵심 데이터는 '금고' 속에 숨겨라" Windows가 장악당하고 서버 펌웨어가 오염되는 최악의 상황, 해커가 관리자 권한으로 소프트웨어 스냅샷을 백업까지 통째로 지우는 순간 기업은 파산합니다.  이때 기업을 구원하는 것은 오직 디포트리스 물리 격리 금고뿐입니다. 네트워크와 물리적으로 완전히 단절된 에어갭(Air-Gap) 상태에서, 개정된 국가 망 보안체계(N2SF)를 준수하는 일방향 데이터 다이오드 전송을 통해 그 어떤 AI 에이전트도 건드릴 수 없는 절대적 불변 백업을 완성합니다.  📷 결 론 사고는 예고 없이 찾아오지만, 기업의 파멸은 막을 수 있습니다. 글로벌 보안 동맹의 정보 소외를 걱정할 시간에, MOMUS AI로 우리 시스템을 먼저 철저하게 털어보고, 핵심 자산은 디포트리스 물리 금고에 숨기십시오.  AI로 공격하는 시대, 방어자 역시 대등한 수준의 AI 무기와 강력한 물리적 방패를 갖춰야만 살아남을 수 있습니다. #디포트리스 #MOMUSAI #Windows취약점 #API보안 #금융권보안 #AI해킹 #미토스 #제로트러스트 #물리격리백업 #사이버레질리언스

외국 뉴스 님만 본다고 생각하지 마세요 미정갤에서 항공모함 국회해산 희망회로 돌리는 글 많이 보나본데 증거가 확실하다해도 동맹국 개입은  한계가 있습니다 상호방호조약에 사이버 공격 조항도 포함되어 있지만 국회해산 같은건 남북이 대치된 상황에서 그리고 우리나라는 법치국가이기때문에 미국이 직접적인 국회 해산은 현실적으로 불가능해요  적국인 베네주엘라도 혼란 야기를 줄이기 위해 마두로 2인자에게 정권을 이양했어요  결정적 증거가 국제적으로 인정되면 상황이 크게 요동치고 미국 압박도 훨씬 세져서 조기대선 가능성이 실질적으로 높아지지만  최종 열쇠는 한국 국회 사법부 여론에 있는데, 국회는 민주당이 다수석이고 사법부는 이미 민주당과 한편입니다  결국 해결할수 있는건  우리 국민들이 깨어나 여론을 일으키는데 한국 국민들은 아직 먹고살만하니 민주당 지지자들이 아직도 깨어나지 못하죠  그래서 미국은 경제제제로 국민들 깨어나게 만들거고 국제 여론을 일으켜 총선 대선에 미국 이익 우선주의에 입각 국방비 인상 대미투자를 확대할 사람으로 앉힐겁니다  미국이 국제적 여론 부담을 덜 느낄수 있는 길은 우리 국민이 깨어나 여론이 폭발해서 우리 손으로 끌어내려야 하는겁니다

sample_tweets.text: 기술 민족주의와 국가 안보'  앤트로픽 미토스의 사이버 위협 공유 네트워크 구축 소식은 빅테크와 정부가 왜 AI 독점에 사활을 거는지 보여주는 완벽한 사례입니다. 📉⚔️  자동화된 대규모 위협:  AI가 소프트웨어 취약점을 찾는 속도가 인간 보안 전문가의 패치 속도를 압도하면서, 금융·방산 등 국가 핵심 인프라가 상시적인 마비 위협에 노출되었습니다.  통제되는 기술, 소외되는 동맹:  미국 중심의 소수 기업(아마존, 마이크로소프트, JP모건 등)만 이 강력한 방패를 공유받고 있으며, 미 정부는 기술 유출을 막고 있습니다.  결국 AI 패권국이 '엠바고'를 걸면 타국은 사이버 방어의 사각지대에 놓이게 됩니다. 대한민국을 비롯한 비미국권 국가들이 독자적인 파운데이션 모델(독파모)과 소버린 AI 인프라를 구축해야 하는 진짜 이유는 여기에 있습니다. 기술 자립 없는 안보는 신기루일 뿐입니다. 🇰🇷🪖  wsj.com/tech/ai/anthropic-le… #소버린AI #앤트로픽 #ClaudeMythos #CyberSecurity #국가안보 #AI패권 #기술자립

"파리 G7 재무장관, 중앙은행 총재 회의 핵심 요약"  '성장 둔화와 에너지 충격이 동시에 오는 상황에서 어떻게 글로벌 시스템을 안정시킬 것인가'가 메인 주제  가장 중요하게 논의된 건 크게 3가지 1. 글로벌 불균형 2. 중동발 에너지 리스크 3. 금융, 재정 안정 대응  + AI 사이버 위협, 희토류 공급망  첫 번째로 G7은 세계 경제의 불균형 문제를 장기적인 시스템 리스크로 바라봤다  경상수지 격차, 성장의 편중, 국가 간 정책 공조 약화가 이어지면서 같은 글로벌 시스템 안에서도 각국의 속도와 방향이 점점 달라지고 있다는 것이다  그래서 회의에서는 개발재원 확대, 다자개발은행 역할 강화, 취약국 지원 같은 자금 조달 메커니즘도 함께 논의됐다  두 번째 핵심은 에너지  중동 긴장으로 유가 변동성이 커지자 G7은 에너지 공급망 안정과 시장 충격 완화에 초점을 맞췄다  미국 역시 러시아산 원유 제재 일부 예외를 연장하며 취약국 원유 접근성과 시장 안정 사이의 균형을 맞추려는 모습을 보였다  세 번째는 금융, 재정 안정 대응  중앙은행들은 성급한 방향 전환보다 “데이터를 보며 판단하겠다”는 입장을 유지했다  이건 유가 상승이 다시 인플레이션을 자극할 가능성과 그에 따른 금리 불확실성을 경계하고 있다는 것이다  이번 G7 회의는 에너지, 물가, 금리, 유동성, 지정학 리스크가 하나의 시스템처럼 연결되어 있다는 점을 다시 한 번 보여줬다  그래서 투자할 때에도 이 전체 시스템을 이해할 수 있어야 한다

AI의 미래는 에이전트 중심입니다.  수십억 개의 AI 에이전트가 등장할 것입니다. 모든 개인과 기업은 여러 개의 에이전트를 보유하게 될 것입니다.  이로 인해 사이버 보안은 주목해야 할 가장 흥미로운 분야 중 하나가 되었습니다.  위협 탐지 $CRWD 크라우드스트라이크(CrowdStrike) $PANW 팔로알토 네트웍스(Palo Alto Networks) $S 센티넬원(SentinelOne)  네트워크 보안 업체 $FTNT 포티넷(Fortinet) $FFIV F5 네트웍스(F5 Networks) $CSCO 시스코 $ATEN A10 네트웍스 $CHKP 체크포인트  클라우드 보안 $NET 클라우드플레어 $ZS Zscaler $OKTA 옥타  생체 인식 신원 확인 $YOU 클리어 시큐어  신원 및 접근 관리 $OKTA 옥타  취약점 관리 $TENB 테네이블 $RPD 래피드7 $SCWX 시큐어웍스  데이터 보안 및 분석 $VRNS 바로니스  IT 서비스 및 컨설팅 $INFY 인포시스 $BAH 부즈 앨런 해밀턴 $LDOS 리도스 $PSN 파슨스  사이버 보안 ETF $HACK 앰플리파이 사이버 보안 ETF $CIBR 퍼스트 트러스트 사이버 보안 ETF

sample_tweets.text: 트럼프 행정부, 첨단 AI 모델 출시 전 정부 선공개 요구하는 행정명령 추진  백악관이 이르면 이번 주 중 사이버 보안 및 AI 안전에 관한 행정명령(EO)을 발표할 계획인 것으로 알려졌다.  소식통에 따르면, 이번 행정명령의 핵심은 첨단 AI 모델에 대한 사이버 보안을 강화하고, AI 개발사들이 신제품 출시 전 정부에 관련 정보를 사전에 알리도록 하는 자발적 프레임워크를 구축하는 것이다.  주요 내용 및 핵심 포인트 1. 자발적 사전 공개 프레임워크 구축: - AI 개발사들이 첨단 AI 모델을 대중에 공식 출시하기 최소 90일 전에 정부 및 주요 인프라 제공업체에 모델을 미리 공유하도록 하는 방안이 포함되었다. - 국가안보 부처와 민간 기관이 협력하여 어떤 모델을 '프론티어 모델(Frontier Model)'로 지정할지 검토하고, 출시 전 위험성을 평가하는 다단계 검토 시스템이 도입될 예정이다.  2. 사이버 보안 전면 강화: - 국방부(펜타곤)를 비롯한 국가안보 기관의 보안을 강화하고, 사이버 전문 인력 채용을 확대한다.  - 병원, 은행 등 국가 주요 인프라의 사이버 시스템을 보호하며, AI 업계와 정부 간의 해킹 및 침해 정보 공유를 활성화하는 내용이 담겼다.  배경 및 행정부 내 기류 변화 트럼프 행정부는 초기까지만 해도 사이버보안 인프라청(CISA)의 예산과 인력을 대폭 감축하는 등 사이버 보안을 최우선 과제로 두지 않았다.  그러나 앤스로픽(Anthropic)의 '미토스(Mythos)'와 오픈AI(OpenAI)의 최신 모델 'GPT-5.5-Cyber' 등 전례 없는 속도로 소프트웨어 취약점을 찾아내고 악용할 수 있는 고성능 AI 모델들이 등장하면서 정부 안팎에서 위기감이 고조되었다. 첨단 AI가 가져올 사이버 위협이 트럼프 행정부의 'AI 무조건적 속도전' 기조를 완화시킨 결정적 계기가 된 것으로 풀이된다.  전망 및 한계 이번 행정명령은 AI 안전성 문제를 다루겠다는 약속을 이행하는 차원이지만, 일각에서 요구해 온 강력한 규제 수준에는 미치지 못한다. 강제성이 없는 '자발적 참여' 방식에 기반하고 있어, 미국 내에서 고조되는 반(反) AI 정서나 대대적인 강력 규제를 외치는 하드라이너들의 목소리를 완전히 잠재우기는 어려울 것이라는 지적이 나온다.  현재 백악관 관계자는 "모든 정책 발표는 대통령이 직접 할 것이며, 행정명령 초안에 대한 논의는 아직 추측일 뿐"이라며 신중한 입장을 유지하고 있다.

최근 샤이훌루드 악성코드의 등장과 솔라 시큐리티의 새로운 보안 플랫폼 출시는 사이버 보안의 중대한 전환점을 암시합니다.  샤이훌루드 악성코드는 npm 생태계를 겨냥한 정보탈취 공격에 사용되었고, 이에 따라 커뮤니티에서는 보안 공격의 정교화에 대한 우려가 커지고 있습니다. 한편, 솔라 시큐리티의 자율형 위험 인텔리전스 플랫폼인 '루미나'는 기존 시스템의 한계를 해결할 수 있을지 기대를 모으고 있지만, 그 효과에 대한 의문도 존재합니다. tinyurl.com/24gw87d3

저 여자는 인생 조졌습니다. 이유는 아래와 같음.  <출처. 구글 AI> 해당 여성이 실제 위장 정보 요원인데 신분이 노출된 상황이라고 가정한다면, 국가 안보 및 외교적 관점에서 중국과 미국으로부터 받게 될 불이익은 다음과 같습니다.  [중국(소속국)으로부터 받을 불이익] - 작전 실패 및 보안 유출 책임 정보 기관원으로서 가장 중요한 자산인 '신분 비닉(Cover)'을 유지하지 못하고 대중에 노출되었기 때문에 내부 징계나 감찰을 피할 수 없습니다.  - 현직 배제 및 보직 박Primary 가치 상실 얼굴과 신원이 소셜미디어를 통해 전 세계에 공개된 요원은 더 이상 해외 공작이나 중요 위장 임무에 투입될 수 없습니다.  따라서 한직으로 좌천되거나 현업에서 영구 배제될 가능성이 매우 높습니다.  - 국가 위신 실추에 따른 문책 국빈 만찬이라는 초민감한 외교 무대에서 정보 활동 정황이 드러나 국가적 망신을 샀다는 이유로 엄중한 사법적 처벌이나 내부 숙청을 당할 수 있습니다.  [미국(상대국)으로부터 받을 불이익] - 기피인물(Persona Non Grata) 지정 및 입국 금지 미국의 국가 원수를 근거리에서 감시·첩보 활동을 한 인물로 식별되므로, 향후 미국 및 우방국으로의 입국이 영구히 금지됩니다.  - 제재 명단(블랙리스트) 등재 미국 재무부나 국무부의 제재 대상 명단에 이름이 올라 미국 내 자산 동결이나 금융 거래 금지 등의 불이익을 받을 수 있습니다.  - 미국 내 활동 시 체포 및 기소 만약 해당 인물이 추후 미국 영토 내에서 다른 신분으로 활동하다 적발될 경우, 미등록 외국 대리인 관리법(FARA) 위반이나 간첩죄 혐의로 연방수사국(FBI)에 의해 체포되어 장기형을 선고받을 수 있습니다.

PowerShell-driven DFIR workflows in 2026 combine memory acquisition, automated analysis, and KAPE-style timelines into scripted pipelines that deliver both speed and forensic defensibility for Windows incident response.  Key workflow components: • Collect-MemoryDump: Orchestrates tools like Belkasoft Live RAM Capturer and WinPMEM with consistent PowerShell interface, hash verification, and predictable output paths • MemProcFS-Analyzer: Mounts RAM dumps as virtual filesystems, runs multi-threaded YARA/ClamAV scans, extracts network artifacts with IP geo-enrichment • Get-MiniTimeline: Wraps KAPE collection and EZ Tools parsing into single script, produces filtered Excel slices and full CSV timelines from MFT/EventLogs/Registry  Modern 4-step workflow: • Remote deployment via EDR live response shells for memory/triage collection • Backend processing with automated mounting, scanning, and artifact extraction to CSV • Timeline generation targeting specific incident windows with cross-reference to execution artifacts • Correlation with documented script versions, command lines, and chain-of-custody for legal defensibility  Implement runtime baselines and completion criteria in SOPs - CPU/disk pressure can cause incomplete results if analysts interrupt collection prematurely. Hash all artifacts at collection and intake.  #DFIR_Radar

Hacker Wars - May 20, 2026  Your daily dose of infosec chaos  ---  GitHub got popped, BitLocker got bypassed, and Grafana's source code walked out the door. Supply chain attacks are the gift that keeps on giving - if by "gift" you mean "incident response nightmares." Three stories, three different ways your trust model just got wrecked.  ---  GitHub Breached - TeamPCP Steals 3,800 Internal Repos Via Malicious VS Code Extension  The TeamPCP hacking group confirmed what many feared: they accessed roughly 3,800 GitHub internal repositories after an employee installed a poisoned VS Code extension. The compromised employee device gave the attackers a foothold into GitHub's internal codebase, including private source code and internal tooling. GitHub says there's no evidence of customer data impact, but the exposure of internal repos is a significant intellectual property and security concern.  **What to do:** Audit your VS Code extensions inventory and implement allowlisting for developer tooling. If you're using GitHub, review your organization's access controls and monitor for anomalous API activity.  ---  Microsoft Drops Mitigation for YellowKey BitLocker Zero-Day (CVE-2026-45585)  Microsoft released a mitigation for YellowKey, a BitLocker security feature bypass vulnerability that carries a CVSS score of 6.8. The zero-day, now tracked as CVE-2026-45585, was publicly disclosed last week and allows attackers to circumvent full-disk encryption protections. Microsoft is aware of active exploitation but a full patch isn't available yet - just a workaround.  **What to do:** Apply the Microsoft mitigation immediately if you rely on BitLocker for endpoint encryption. Consider layering additional encryption controls and monitor for physical access indicators on high-value endpoints.  ---  Grafana Breach Deepens - TanStack npm Attack Vector Exposed  Grafana Labs confirmed that its recent GitHub breach, initially disclosed on May 19, involved a compromised npm package in the TanStack supply chain. The attackers leveraged the poisoned dependency to gain access to Grafana's GitHub environment, exfiltrating both public and private source code. Grafana says customer production systems and data were not affected, but the source code exposure could fuel future vulnerability research.  **What to do:** If you use Grafana products, pin your dependencies and monitor for security advisories. Review your software supply chain security posture and consider using tools like Sigstore or SLSA to verify package integrity.  ---  Catch you tomorrow. Big moment for Nextron, and for me personally  The first version of our scanner ran in December 2012. Back then it was still a tool for consulting and incident response cases. We founded @nextronsystems in 2017 with around 35 customers. Today it’s more than 550.  Most of that growth happened organically. Word of mouth, customer trust, posts on Twitter, and a lot of steady work on the product.  Maybe a fairly German way to build a company: spend what you earn, focus on the substance, care more about what’s inside the box than the box itself.  We were not always great at explaining that box, though. Sometimes we were probably a bit clumsy in how we communicated what the products can do and where they fit best 🙂  That also means there is still a lot we can improve around the product: clearer communication, better channels, better integrations, better support and a more professional setup for international growth.  That’s why I’m genuinely happy that Eurazeo / Elevate is joining us for the next phase.  Nextron has always been strongest where standard tools have blind spots: forensic scanning, compromise assessment, unusual systems, backup data, forensic images and environments where you cannot just install another agent.  Thanks to the Nextron team, our customers, partners and everyone who helped us get here.  Now we keep building.  linkedin.com/posts/nextron-s…

Hackers are now using the same AI tools you use every day.  Google's Threat Intelligence Group just dropped a massive report confirming what we all feared.  Here's what it says in plain English:  → A criminal group used an AI model to discover a zero-day vulnerability that traditional security scanners completely missed. They were planning a mass attack before Google caught it.  → An Android malware called PROMPTSPY uses the Gemini API to autonomously control your phone. It simulates touch gestures, captures your biometric data, and when you try to uninstall it, it places an invisible overlay over the "Uninstall" button so you literally can't tap it.  → Russia-linked threat actors are using AI to write fake "decoy code" inside their malware. Security scanners read the code, think it's a normal program, and let it through.  → China and North Korea-linked groups are sending thousands of automated prompts to AI models to mass-analyze vulnerabilities and build entire exploit arsenals at a speed that would be impossible with human researchers alone.  → Threat actors have built full middleware systems to anonymously pool and rotate stolen premium accounts across Gemini, Claude, and OpenAI. They're running the same tools you pay for, at scale, for free.  These aren't basement hackers with custom military software. They're using the exact same AI products sitting in your browser tabs right now.  The full report is worth your time if you work in tech, security, or build with AI.

The South Korean tanker's cautious passage through Hormuz is a useful diplomatic pause — but it shouldn't lull the world into thinking the real threat is receding.  Behind the scenes, Iran still retains the ability to reconstitute its nuclear capabilities in smaller, clandestine facilities, effectively curtailing IAEA oversight. That's the assessment from Carnegie Endowment: two wars later, the nuclear question remains firmly on the table.  The IAEA hasn't found evidence of active weaponization — yet. But under the JCPOA framework, the benchmark was clear: even with declared enrichment facilities, Tehran needed a minimum of one year to produce weapons-grade material. Strip away oversight, move operations underground, and that timeline compresses dramatically.  While the world watches oil tankers and negotiates passage terms, the regime is quietly preserving the option it values most — a nuclear deterrent that could reshape the entire region.  That's why the endgame can't be another round of temporary de-escalation. It has to be regime accountability. @PahlaviReza has laid out the only path that addresses both the nuclear threat and the human catastrophe: immediate dismantling of the nuclear program, recognition of Israel, and the Cyrus Accords uniting a free Iran with the region.  The tanker may pass today. But without addressing what's happening beneath the surface, the next crisis won't be about shipping lanes — it'll be about what Tehran builds while the world looks away.  #IranRevolution2026 #FreeIran

🚨A data broker marketplace listing published on May 18, 2026, claims to offer 3.3 million customer registration records belonging to Deutsche Telekom, Germany’s largest telecommunications provider. The post advertises a comprehensive database export containing highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), financial details, and contract data, with escrow services accepted for transactions.  According to the listing, the dataset includes fields such as full names, dates of birth, national identification numbers, email addresses, phone numbers, physical addresses, and International Bank Account Numbers (IBANs). The accompanying JSON sample reveals structured customer profiles with detailed contract specifics, including tariff types, monthly pricing, subscription start and end dates, and banking connection details. The seller directs potential buyers to a Telegram channel for samples, pricing, and negotiation.  Deutsche Telekom AG operates one of the world’s largest telecom networks, serving over 300 million customers globally and employing approximately 250,000 people. As a designated critical infrastructure operator in Germany, any compromise of its customer database carries significant implications for data privacy, financial security, and regulatory compliance.  While the marketplace post asserts the authenticity and volume of the leak, independent verification remains pending. Data broker listings frequently circulate on underground forums, sometimes featuring outdated, partially fabricated, or aggregated records. Cybersecurity analysts recommend treating such listings as high-risk indicators, particularly given the inclusion of active IBANs and national ID numbers, which could facilitate identity theft, account takeover attacks, and targeted phishing campaigns.  The exposure of financial account details alongside comprehensive PII elevates the threat level substantially. Security experts advise Deutsche Telekom to investigate potential internal access logs, third-party vendor compromises, or API misconfigurations that may have facilitated the data exfiltration. Customers are encouraged to monitor bank statements, review credit reports, and consider enhanced identity protection measures while the incident is under review.  #CyberSecurity #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #DarkWeb #TelecomSecurity #IdentityTheft

🚨A hacker collective operating under the name "EsqueleSquad" has claimed responsibility for a large-scale data breach affecting multiple major Argentine government, financial, and healthcare institutions. According to a public forum post and associated Telegram channel, the group alleges it has obtained and is releasing over 33 million records containing highly sensitive personal, financial, and medical data.  The alleged leak primarily targets three entities: the Banco Central de la República Argentina (BCRA), the Instituto de Obra Social Municipal de la Ciudad de Buenos Aires (IOMA), and the Policía Federal Argentina (PFA, now restructured as the Policía de la Nación Argentina). Additionally, the group claims to possess a private dossier on Axel Kicillof, the Governor of Buenos Aires Province.  According to the breach details, the BCRA dataset contains approximately 32 million credit scoring records. Sample data provided by the group includes national identification numbers (DNI), tax IDs (CUIT), birth dates, and detailed debt information linked to major Argentine financial institutions such as Banco Santander Argentina and Banco de la Provincia de Buenos Aires.  The IOMA dataset reportedly includes over 1 million affiliate records. The exposed data features full names, residential addresses, membership numbers, email addresses, and highly sensitive medical information, including diagnosed chronic conditions (e.g., diabetes, hypercholesterolemia) and prescribed medications (e.g., atorvastatin, bisoprolol). IOMA serves as the municipal social security and healthcare provider for Buenos Aires City, covering millions of residents.  Furthermore, the group claims to have leaked 903 classified PDF documents from the national police force, alongside a personal dossier on Governor Kicillof containing phone numbers, chat logs, residential addresses, and credit scoring data.  "EsqueleSquad" states that the data is being released for free via Telegram and a third-party file-hosting service, with promises of additional dumps if the thread receives community support. As of now, neither the BCRA, IOMA, nor the Argentine government has issued an official confirmation or incident report regarding the breach. Cybersecurity professionals typically advise treating such dark web and forum claims with caution until independent verification or official disclosure occurs. However, the scale and sensitivity of the alleged exposure—spanning national financial infrastructure, public healthcare, and law enforcement—would represent a critical cybersecurity incident if verified.  If confirmed, the breach would directly impact millions of Argentine citizens, exposing their financial standing, medical histories, and personal identifiers. Authorities and affected institutions are expected to monitor the situation closely and may initiate forensic investigations, credit monitoring alerts, and data protection reviews in accordance with Argentina's Personal Data Protection Law.  #CyberSecurity #DataBreach #Argentina #ThreatIntelligence #CyberThreats #DarkWeb #DataLeak #HealthcareSecurity #FinancialSecurity #GovernmentBreach #CTI #ThreatMon #InfoSec #CyberAttack #IdentityTheft #DigitalRisk #BCRA #IOMA #PFA

🇦🇺 A threat actor is advertising an alleged dataset tied to an Australian membership and club management platform, claiming exposure of approximately 485,000 records associated with miclub.com.au.  According to the underground forum listing, the dataset allegedly contains: • Member contact records • Event and booking information • Membership payment and billing data • Club participation history • Internal membership management metadata  The post claims the exposed fields include: • Full names • Email addresses • Mobile and home phone numbers • Residential addresses • Dates of birth • Membership status and history • Event booking activity • Payment records and invoice metadata • Emergency contact details • Billing addresses • Membership tiers and points • Internal comments and communication preferences  If authentic, this type of dataset would have substantial value to cybercriminals because it combines: • Identity information • Financial metadata • Behavioral activity • Social/community participation patterns  Membership platforms are increasingly attractive targets because they often centralize: • Payment ecosystems • Loyalty programs • Event participation • Personal communications • Geographic data • Long-term customer histories  One particularly notable aspect is the inclusion of: • Event bookings • Tee time participation • Membership renewal data • Emergency contacts  That operational context can significantly improve the effectiveness of: • Social engineering campaigns • Spear phishing attacks • Account impersonation • Fraud targeting affluent demographics • Physical-world profiling risks  Threat actors frequently prioritize lifestyle and membership ecosystems because they can reveal: • Travel habits • Recreational routines • Social affiliations • Spending behaviors • Location patterns • High-net-worth targeting opportunities  The exposure of payment and billing-related metadata also raises concerns around: • Financial fraud • Subscription abuse • Invoice phishing • Credential reuse attacks • Synthetic identity fraud  At this stage, the claims remain publicly unverified. However, organizations operating membership ecosystems should review: • Payment system segmentation • Customer data retention policies • Access controls for CRM platforms • Third-party integrations • Logging and monitoring capabilities • Dark web monitoring for reposted datasets  This incident highlights how non-traditional platforms — including recreational, hospitality, and membership ecosystems — continue to become high-value targets due to the richness of the personal and behavioral data they store.  🇦🇺 #DDW #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #Australia #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #Privacy #OSINT #CyberCrime

🇦🇷 A threat actor is advertising an alleged dataset tied to Argentina’s judicial infrastructure, claiming exposure of approximately 478,000 records associated with intranet.jus.mendoza.gov.ar.  According to the underground forum post, the dataset allegedly contains: • Judicial personnel records • Legal case participant information • Internal communication logs • Contact and identity information • Operational metadata tied to case management workflows  The exposed fields reportedly include: • National ID numbers • Full names • Personal and work email addresses • Mobile and home phone numbers • Job titles and departmental assignments • Home/work addresses • Communication history • Case participation metadata • Internal status and escalation fields • Confidentiality/security clearance indicators  If authentic, this type of breach would represent a serious risk not only to privacy, but also to judicial integrity and institutional security.  Judicial and legal-sector datasets are uniquely sensitive because they can expose: • Judges and court personnel • Prosecutors and investigators • Witnesses or case participants • Internal communications • Escalation chains • Sensitive legal workflows • Operational security structures  One particularly concerning aspect is the inclusion of communication logs and participant metadata. Those records can enable: • Targeted intimidation campaigns • Social engineering against judicial staff • Phishing targeting legal professionals • Exposure of sensitive case relationships • Mapping of internal judicial operations • Identification of high-value legal targets  The reference to “security clearance” and confidentiality-related fields could also increase the intelligence value of the dataset for: • Organized cybercriminal groups • Nation-state actors • Corruption-focused intelligence operations • Influence or extortion campaigns  Modern attacks against government legal systems increasingly focus on: • Operational disruption • Intelligence collection • Long-term institutional surveillance • Credential harvesting • Exposure of politically sensitive information  The broader concern is that judicial ecosystems often connect to: • Government identity systems • Law enforcement databases • Citizen records • Internal administrative infrastructure • Inter-agency communication platforms  At this stage, the claims remain publicly unverified. However, organizations operating judicial or legal-sector systems should prioritize: • Identity and access management reviews • Segmentation of legal case systems • Monitoring for credential reuse • Internal communication security • Dark web monitoring for reposts or samples • Insider-threat and privileged-access reviews  This incident highlights how government and judicial infrastructures continue to be attractive targets for both financially motivated and intelligence-driven threat actors.  🇦🇷 #DDW #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #Argentina #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #GovernmentSecurity #Privacy #CyberCrime

🇮🇳 A threat actor is claiming exposure of approximately 7 million Indian banking customer records allegedly tied to Bank of India.  While the authenticity of the claims has not been publicly verified, a dataset of this scale involving a major financial institution would represent a significant cybersecurity and fraud risk.  Banking datasets are among the most valuable commodities in underground markets because they enable: • Financial fraud • Identity theft • SIM swapping • Account takeover attempts • Social engineering campaigns • Loan and credit fraud • Money laundering facilitation • Large-scale phishing operations  In many modern banking-related breaches, attackers are not only targeting: • Customer identities • Account numbers • Contact information  but also operational metadata such as: • Transaction histories • KYC documentation • Device identifiers • Authentication workflows • Internal banking processes  If legitimate, a dataset involving millions of records could have cascading effects across: • Financial institutions • Telecom providers • Payment ecosystems • Government identity systems • Third-party fintech platforms  India’s rapidly expanding digital banking ecosystem makes large-scale financial datasets especially attractive to cybercriminal groups due to: • Massive customer volume • Mobile-first banking adoption • UPI/payment ecosystem integration • Expanding fintech connectivity • High credential reuse rates  Threat actors frequently weaponize banking datasets through: • Credential stuffing attacks • Voice phishing (vishing) • Fake banking portals • OTP interception campaigns • Malware targeting financial users • AI-assisted fraud and impersonation  One major concern in modern financial breaches is that attackers increasingly combine: • Banking data • Telecom data • Government identity data • Behavioral metadata  to create highly accurate fraud profiles capable of bypassing traditional verification systems.  Organizations in the financial sector should continuously monitor for: • Underground forum listings • Credential reuse attempts • Unusual authentication activity • SIM swap indicators • Dark web mentions of customer datasets • Exposure of internal banking infrastructure  Recommended defensive actions include: • Immediate credential resets where applicable • MFA enforcement • Transaction anomaly monitoring • Device fingerprinting validation • Enhanced fraud detection rules • Customer awareness notifications • Third-party vendor reviews  This also reinforces the growing importance of treating financial-sector cybersecurity as a national critical infrastructure issue rather than only an enterprise security problem.  🇮🇳 #DDW #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #India #Banking #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #FinancialSecurity #CyberCrime

🇩🇿 A threat actor is advertising an alleged dataset tied to Algeria’s cultural asset and protected property management sector, claiming exposure of approximately 425,000 records associated with ogebc.com.  According to the underground forum listing, the dataset allegedly contains structured operational and customer-related information divided into: • Customer/account records • Order and transaction history • Support ticket and interaction data  The post claims the exposed fields include: • Full names • Email addresses • Phone and fax numbers • Postal/shipping addresses • Account metadata • Purchase/order history • Payment-related information • Support interactions and internal notes • Tracking and delivery information • Customer segmentation and sales metadata  One of the more concerning aspects is the aggregation of multiple business functions into a single dataset. When attackers obtain: • CRM data • Order history • Support records • Internal workflow metadata  they can reconstruct a highly detailed operational picture of an organization and its customers.  If authentic, this type of exposure creates elevated risks for: • Targeted phishing campaigns • Business email compromise (BEC) • Fraud against vendors/customers • Supply-chain reconnaissance • Identity theft • Social engineering using historical support interactions  The inclusion of support-ticket data is particularly valuable to adversaries because support systems often contain: • Internal escalation notes • Employee names • Operational procedures • Sensitive attachments • Authentication troubleshooting details • Customer behavioral patterns  Threat actors increasingly monetize “business intelligence datasets” rather than only credential dumps. These structured datasets can be leveraged for: • Long-term intelligence gathering • Commercial fraud • Reconnaissance of critical sectors • Relationship mapping between organizations and customers  The reference to Algeria’s “national cultural asset management” ecosystem also raises broader concerns around: • Public-sector digital security maturity • Third-party vendor exposure • Protection of historically or culturally sensitive records • Centralized citizen/business data storage  At this stage, the claims remain unverified publicly. However, organizations handling operational and customer ecosystems should review: • CRM exposure risks • Ticketing platform security • Access control policies • Data retention practices • Third-party integrations • Monitoring for underground reposts and samples  This incident highlights how modern breaches increasingly expose not just identities, but the operational context surrounding individuals and institutions.  🇩🇿 #DDW #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #Algeria #Privacy #OSINT #CyberCrime

🇦🇷 A threat actor is advertising an alleged dataset tied to a dog services and booking platform in Argentina, claiming exposure of approximately 624,000 records associated with rucabarukboxer.com.ar.  According to the forum post, the dataset allegedly contains three major categories of information:  • Customer/contact records • Dog profile and breeder information • Service booking and appointment data  The exposed fields reportedly include: • Full names • National ID numbers • Email addresses • Phone numbers • Usernames • Physical location data • Birthdates • Emergency contacts • Booking/payment metadata • Veterinary and vaccination-related information tied to animals  One notable aspect of this alleged leak is the depth of profiling. Unlike typical credential dumps, this dataset appears to combine: • Personal identity information • Behavioral/service usage data • Household/location metadata • Animal ownership records • Appointment histories  That combination significantly increases the intelligence value of the dataset for: • Social engineering • Targeted phishing • Identity fraud • Credential stuffing campaigns • Physical targeting/stalking risks • Profiling of affluent households or breeders  The inclusion of pet-related operational data is particularly sensitive because pet ownership databases often contain: • Home addresses • Travel schedules • Emergency contact patterns • Routine behavioral information • High-trust customer relationships  Threat actors increasingly target niche platforms because smaller organizations often: • Have weaker security maturity • Store highly centralized customer data • Lack proper segmentation • Retain excessive historical records • Operate without mature monitoring capabilities  The post also demonstrates how underground actors are evolving from simple “email/password” sales toward structured intelligence datasets that provide contextual information useful for long-term fraud and profiling operations.  At this stage, the claims remain unverified publicly. However, organizations operating customer-service ecosystems should treat combined operational/customer datasets as high-risk assets requiring: • Data minimization • Encryption at rest • Strict retention policies • Access segmentation • Continuous monitoring • Third-party security reviews  This is another reminder that even highly specialized platforms can become attractive targets once they accumulate rich customer ecosystems and operational metadata.  #DDW #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #DataBreach #ThreatIntelligence #Argentina #Privacy #OSINT #CyberCrime

🚨 A threat actor operating under the “TeamPCP” identity is claiming to sell alleged internal source code and organizational data tied to GitHub.  According to the underground forum post, the actor claims: • Approximately 4,000 private repositories are included • Internal organizational data is allegedly accessible • Samples are available for verification • The data is being sold rather than used for ransomware/extortion • A minimum asking price above $50,000 is requested • The actor threatens public release if no buyer emerges  At this stage, there is no public confirmation that the claims are authentic.  However, if legitimate, exposure of internal repositories at this scale could create severe downstream risks including: • Exposure of proprietary source code • Secret/API token leakage • Internal infrastructure mapping • Authentication workflow disclosure • CI/CD pipeline compromise • Supply-chain attack opportunities • Discovery of unpatched internal vulnerabilities • Access to developer tooling and automation systems  The reference to “internal orgs” is particularly concerning because compromise of organizational repositories may expose: • Employee metadata • Internal communications • Deployment configurations • Security tooling references • Enterprise customer integrations • Cloud architecture details  Historically, source-code leaks have enabled: • Rapid weaponization of vulnerabilities • Credential harvesting • Development of phishing infrastructure • Reverse engineering of internal services • Long-term supply-chain persistence  The threat actor’s framing that this is “not ransomware” aligns with a growing trend where financially motivated actors increasingly: • Monetize direct access • Sell code privately • Auction sensitive repositories • Trade insider-style intelligence • Avoid traditional encryption/extortion operations  This model is attractive to adversaries because source code itself can be more valuable than ransom payments, especially for: • Nation-state actors • Competitor intelligence operations • Advanced persistent threats (APTs) • Supply-chain attackers  Organizations should treat large-scale repository compromise as a potential enterprise-wide incident because modern repositories often contain: • Infrastructure-as-Code • Cloud secrets • Deployment automation • Embedded credentials • Third-party integration keys • Security architecture documentation  Recommended defensive actions include: • Immediate secret rotation • Repository access review • Token invalidation • Dependency integrity checks • CI/CD audit reviews • Git history scanning for exposed credentials • Monitoring underground communities for reposts/samples  This incident also reinforces the increasing strategic importance of: • Secure SDLC programs • Repository segmentation • Just-in-time privileged access • Secrets management • Supply-chain security monitoring  #DDW #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #DarkWeb #GitHub #SourceCode #SupplyChainSecurity sample_tweets.text: I had some people DM me and tag me on this post to determine if it's malware or "slop".  Using the software requires providing billing information prior to downloading the trial. I got mildly annoyed by this and contacted support. I requested access to the binary without needing to provide billing information.  Their support team was ... actually very, very, very polite and nice. I was kind of taken back by how polite they were. They provided me the software with a 60 day trial. I can't tell if they know I do malware development and reverse engineering because (usually) places are hesitant to just give me the stuff like this on a platter.  I would feel bad if I was hyper-critical of this product because of how polite the person running this profile is, they're just a chill dude.  To be direct: - Is this malware? No. - Is this slop? Probably not, no. - Does this actually improve FPS? Yes, unironically. However, it is very important you realize this software is changing the voltage and clock speed on your machine (among other things). They're achieving this in a legitimate way by working with AMD and Intel with actual SDK (Software Development Kit) documentation.  This product went to great lengths to secure its source code. It has junk code insertion, in-memory patching (stubs), junk variables, control flow obfuscation, and it also does device finger-printing to ensure you don't steal their product. All of this was performed using professional anti-reverse engineering products.  It was a real pain in my balls to deal with. I got mildly irritated at several points. Some strings are AES256 encrypted and decrypted when needed (run-time lazy loading) making static-analysis even more difficult.  Despite all of this, none of it is malicious. They just don't want nerds stealing their stuff. At first glance however it does use methods similar to malware to avoid reverse engineering.  The application UI is also incredibly heavy. It is using the latest and great .NET UI stuff to make it look super cool and gamer-like. It launches from HyperTune.exe which then loads the actual (super obfuscated) HyperTune.dll using HOSTFXR (Google it). The obfuscation tools they used disassembled and fractionated the application entry point (and subsequent functionality) down into 1,618 other functions (see attached image)  The only saving grace was the visibility into it's dependencies and other 3rd party libraries it uses (Realm for local settings savings, Sentry for logging errors, SimpleInjector for handling classes they use, etc).  I won't go into full details on how their product actually works, I would feel bad because of support dude being a chill dude, but here is my main criticism:  - Loading of kernel-mode drivers from vendors for overclocking. They load AMD and Intel drivers based on your hardware profile on your machine. However, the driver configuration settings are set to AUTOSTART. Hence, once you use this software these kernel-mode components will auto-start even if HyperTune is not running. Additionally, uninstalling HyperTune will not uninstall these kernel-mode components. These kernel-mode components come by default with the installer in a directory called /3p/ but move to SYSTEM32 after installation (as they should be).  - For reasons I do not understand, HyperTune modifies HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WindowsUpdate\AU ... it disables automatic updates from Windows. I don't know why. My presumption is this could prevent potential driver conflicts, but if not managed correctly this exposes users to security vulnerabilities.   Did they actually spend $1,000,000 developing this? With a full development team, infrastructure they're using (Sentry, VERCEL, enterprise and professional anti-reverse engineering tools, etc) ...maybe...?
